title: NA-ULIP
word_text:
How do ULIPs help in achieving Long-Term Financial Goals?
Before buying any financial product, it is required to evaluate, “How does this financial product would serve me?” The first step to answering this question may be, is to understand the nuances of the said financial product. What does it offer? How does it work? How to accomplish long-term goals with this product?
A ULIP is one such financial product that you might consider putting your money into, only after understanding the policy features, benefits, inclusions & exclusions. Buying a ULIP policy is one of the popular choices, especially considering that the plans offer life insurance and investment benefits bundled into a single policy.
But the reason for buying the policy may not be the same for everyone. Hence, before purchasing a ULIP, it is important to take the time to understand why you are buying it and what goals it could help you achieve. When buying any such plan, remember to first distinguish between your short-term goals and long-term goals. With ULIPs, it is necessary to understand the definition of long-term goals, as well as short-term ones, so that you may better organise your investments and financial products.
Another important thing to do before you buy a ULIP is to use a ULIP returns calculator to get an idea of what sort of returns can you expect from the policy.
Unit-Linked Insurance Plans
Before we dive into how these investments may help you  achieve long-term financial goals, let’s start with understanding what a ULIP is. The term Unit-linked Insurance Plan (ULIP) is used to define a type of policy that offers a life cover and an investment component. In the insurance component, the life assured is provided with life insurance coverage. If something unfortunate were to happen to you being the life assured, during the plan’s term, your loved ones would be compensated with a death benefit, subject to the terms and conditions stated in the policy. This money may be used by them to manage the daily cost of living while fulfilling goals as well. On the other hand, you have the market linked wealth creation aspect of the plan. This is where the answer to your concern, “how to achieve long-term financial goals with these plans?”, lies. The wealth creation aspect can help you create a market linked corpus to achieve your dreams and be able to support your family. It invests your money into different market linked funds available with the plan, which might help you earn market linked returns in the long term. Once you have a clear definition of long-term goals, you may be able to see how ULIPs can aid you in the pursuit of your dreams.
Are ULIPs Right For You?
After getting a fairly clear idea of how plans like ULIPs may help you achieve long-term financial goals, the next step may be is to understand what type of investor  are you?. Here are some questions you may ask yourself on this journey, and how they can help you understand whether a ULIP plan can be suitable for  you.
“What is my current financial situation like ?”
One should invest only after ensuring that they are not putting themselves and their family through any financial distress today. A ULIP returns calculator can help you get an estimate of how much will you need to invest into the plan over the next few years if you decide to buy one.
“Am I looking for short-term or long-term gains?”
If we talk about  the basics of a ULIP policy, you need to know that these plans tend to offer long-term market linked returns rather than short-term gains. Hence, if you are looking to be in it for the long haul, you may consider opting for a ULIP.
“Am I willing to take some risk? How much?”
ULIPs are plans that invest your money in market linked funds. These investments are prone to market vulnerabilities. If you have a lower risk appetite, you may choose to invest in debt funds in ULIPs.
“What are my long-term financial goals?”
Buying any financial product should be done with a financial goal in mind and knowing your long-term goals’ definition. What do you want to achieve in the long term? Based on the answer to this, and a keen understanding of ULIPs, you can decide whether you want to choose ULIPs as the policy for your long-term financial growth.
Goals ULIPs Can Help You With
A person  might have short-term goals and long-term goals to fulfil. Investments should be planned based on the type of goals you have. ULIPs may help if you are keen to know how to accomplish long-term goals. Here are a few long-term goals that Unit-linked Insurance Plans may help you achieve.
Retirement
A financially stable retirement might  be a goal for many people across the world. There are various ways to achieve this. One of them is to buy a ULIP in the early years of your career. It helps in creating a market linked corpus with time and can help in keeping your family’s financial future secure.
Children’s education
If you are shouldering the responsibility of your little one’s education, you may want to make provisions for the same early on. ULIP is one of the plans that  offers dual protection of Insurance as well as Investment and may support your child, helping them fulfil their educational dreams. You may consider buying the plan soon after the child is born and have it mature when your child may be closer to starting their higher education.
Buying a house
Moving into a house they own is a dream for many. ULIPs are one of the tools that may help you fulfil this. You can use a ULIP calculator to understand how much you need to put into the plan to get the returns you want and in the duration you want.
Buying assets
If you have long-term goals, such as buying a car, a second home, or renovating your current place, you will need to start saving for it today..
As stated above, buying a ULIP is preferably  a long-term commitment if you want your plan to earn  market linked returns. When buying the plan, keep your long-term goals in your mind and plan accordingly. Try to maintain your ULIP for the originally decided duration. Invest wisely and consult an expert to have all your queries and doubts answered.
Disclaimer:
IN THIS POLICY, THE INVESTMENT RISK IN INVESTMENT PORTFOLIO IS BORNE BY THE POLICYHOLDER. Investment in ULIPs is subject to risks associated with the capital markets. The policy holder is solely responsible for his/her decisions while investing in ULIPs. The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale.
